item.title: History comes home as Fijian Drua Women host first Super Rugby Women’s grand final in Ba
item.description: History will be made this Saturday when the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for the first time, with the club calling on fans from across the country to be part of the occasion. Fijian Drua Head of Commercial and Marketing Shane Hussein says the final is not only a milestone for the club but for Fiji, as it marks the first time the country will host
item.content:encoded:
History will be made this Saturday when the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for the first time, with the club calling on fans from across the country to be part of the occasion.

Fijian Drua Head of Commercial and Marketing Shane Hussein says the final is not only a milestone for the club but for Fiji, as it marks the first time the country will host the competition's decider.

Hussein says although the Drua finished at the top of the table in 2022, they were not eligible to host the grand final because the competition was then exclusively Australian.

He says Rugby Australia has since recognised the success of the Drua Women, allowing the team to earn the right to host the final by finishing at the top of the table and winning their semi-final.

Insert: Shane - Be part of history July 22

He says the final will be played as a double-header, with the Ba and Lautoka women's teams featuring in the curtain-raiser, making the venue's infrastructure an important consideration.

Hussein stressed that while Lautoka has become the Drua Women's fortress, the club wanted to give Ba the opportunity to host such a significant match and gain experience staging major rugby events.

The grand final kicks off at 1pm on Saturday at 4R Stadium in Ba, with tickets priced at $5, $10 and $15.

item.title: Lelean to face RKS for Lutubula Trophy
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026 The prestigious Lutubula Trophy will be up for grabs this weekend as favourites Lelean Memorial School take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the final of the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Under-18 Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026

The prestigious Lutubula Trophy will be up for grabs this weekend as favourites Lelean Memorial School take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the final of the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Under-18 Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition.

The Lutubula Trophy , once regarded as the symbol of dominance among Eastern Zone rugby powerhouses such as Queen Victoria School, Ratu Kadavulevu School and Lelean Memorial School, also serves as part of the qualification pathway to the Vodafone Deans competition.

Lelean heads into the final unbeaten this season and has been the dominant force in the Eastern Zone competition.

Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School surrendered the title after a closely contested semi-final loss to Ratu Kadavulevu School last weekend.

RKS, the reigning Vodafone Deans Under-18 champions, will be looking to add another trophy to their cabinet while also securing a direct pathway to the national quarter-finals.

In the other finals, Queen Victoria School will face Ratu Kadavulevu School in the Under-14, Under-16 and Under-17 grades. Lelean Memorial School will take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the Under-15 and Under-19 finals.

The Eastern Zone finals will be held at HFC Bank Stadium this Saturday.

item.title: Kumar calls for Fijian support ahead of Junior Bula Boys' semi-final clash
item.description: Junior Bula Boys right midfielder Navitesh Kumar is calling on Fijians to continue backing the team as they prepare to face the Solomon Islands in the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final today. Following their final pool match, the Labasa youngster praised his teammates for their commitment, saying they have fought hard and given their all in every match.
item.content:encoded:
Junior Bula Boys right midfielder Navitesh Kumar is calling on Fijians to continue backing the team as they prepare to face the Solomon Islands in the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final today.

Following their final pool match, the Labasa youngster praised his teammates for their commitment, saying they have fought hard and given their all in every match.

He also acknowledged the efforts of the coaching and technical staff, thanking them for their work behind the scenes.

Kumar also thanked the families and supporters who have been following the team's journey from Fiji.

The Junior Bula Boys beat New Caledonia 2–1, thrashed Samoa 5–0 and defeated PNG 3–0 in their final group match to top the pool.

Fiji will play the Solomon Islands in the semi-final at the PNG Football Stadium at 6pm, while New Zealand will face PNG in the other semi-final at 9.30pm.

The final will be played at 9.30pm this Saturday.

The winner and runner-up will qualify for the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2027.

item.title: Family struggles fuel Rainibogi's Commonwealth Games drive
item.description: Rainibogi, who is originally from Levuka in Ovalau, says he is determined to improve on the bronze medal he won at the last Commonwealth Games. He says training has gone well, and he is feeling confident ahead of the competition.
item.content:encoded:
Rainibogi, who is originally from Levuka in Ovalau, says he is determined to improve on the bronze medal he won at the last Commonwealth Games.

He says training has gone well, and he is feeling confident ahead of the competition.

Rainibogi says his goal is simple: to return home with a medal better than bronze.

The national weightlifter says his biggest inspiration is his family, who continue to support him despite facing financial difficulties. He says their sacrifices motivate him to keep working hard and chase his dream of representing Fiji on the international stage.

Rainibogi attended Levuka Public School before completing his secondary education at John Wesley College .

Before taking up weightlifting, Rainibogi played rugby at school but says he discovered his passion for the sport and has never looked back.

He will be one of two Fijian weightlifters competing at the 23rd Commonwealth Games, alongside Apakuki Tabuawaiwai.

Rainibogi is scheduled to compete in the Men's 110kg final on Thursday, 30th July from 8.30pm to 11pm.

The 23rd Commonwealth Games will be held in Glasgow from Saturday, 25th July to Monday, 3rd August.

item.title: Fernandez proud of Argentina despite 1-0 World Cup final defeat
item.description: Argentina midfielder Enzo Fernandez says the team represented their country "in the best possible way" despite losing 1-0 to Spain after extra time in the World Cup final. Fernandez, who was shown a red card after receiving two yellow cards in the final, was sent off during extra time as Argentina pushed for an equaliser.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina midfielder Enzo Fernandez says the team represented their country "in the best possible way" despite losing 1-0 to Spain after extra time in the World Cup final.

Fernandez, who was shown a red card after receiving two yellow cards in the final, was sent off during extra time as Argentina pushed for an equaliser.

The Chelsea midfielder says it was a privilege to be part of a group that always represented Argentina with pride, humility and commitment, adding that they gave everything for the jersey and never gave up.

He did not mention his sending off in his statement, instead choosing to focus on the team's effort and unity throughout the tournament.

Argentina fell just short of retaining the World Cup title after Spain secured a 1-0 victory in extra time.

item.title: Joji Nasova to remain with Fijian Drua after Newcastle Red Bulls release him from contract
item.description: Fijian winger Joji Nasova will remain with the Fijian Drua after Newcastle Red Bulls agreed to release him from his contract for next season due to personal reasons. Nasova had agreed to join the English club ahead of the 2026/27 campaign.
item.content:encoded:
Fijian winger Joji Nasova will remain with the Fijian Drua after Newcastle Red Bulls agreed to release him from his contract for next season due to personal reasons.

Nasova had agreed to join the English club ahead of the 2026/27 campaign.

However, Newcastle Red Bulls says after carefully considering the circumstances, the club agreed to allow Nasova to remain with the Drua.

The club says it worked closely with Nasova, the Fijian Drua and the Fiji Rugby Players Association to reach a resolution that recognises his personal circumstances while respecting contractual stability within the Rugby Football Union and World Rugby frameworks.

Newcastle Red Bulls General Manager Rugby, Neil McIlroy says while the club was looking forward to welcoming Nasova, it fully understands and respects the unique personal circumstances behind the decision.

McIlroy says contractual certainty is important for clubs, players and the wider game, but rugby is also built on people, families and communities, and the club believes its responsibility as an employer extends beyond the field.

He wished Nasova, his family and the Fijian Drua every success in the future.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys taking it one step at a time ahead of OFC U-16 semifinal
item.description: Junior Bula Boys head coach Sunil Kumar says his side is taking one step at a time approach as they prepare for the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semifinal after finishing the group stage strongly. Kumar says his players have continued to grow in confidence with each match, but believes there is still plenty of room for improvement.
item.content:encoded:
Junior Bula Boys head coach Sunil Kumar says his side is taking one step at a time approach as they prepare for the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semifinal after finishing the group stage strongly.

Kumar says his players have continued to grow in confidence with each match, but believes there is still plenty of room for improvement.

He says the young squad can sometimes get carried away by the atmosphere and emotions of the tournament, and they still need to improve their ball possession and overall performance.

Despite that, Kumar says the boys have shown great character throughout the group stage, where they won all three matches to book a place in the semifinals.

He says the coaching staff will now assess injuries, analyse their performances from the three pool matches and prepare the team for the semifinal.

Fiji will play Solomon Islands in the semi-final tomorrow at the PNG Football Stadium at 6pm, while New Zealand will face PNG in the other semi-final at 9.30pm.

The final will be played at 9.30pm this Saturday.

The winner and runner-up will qualify for the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2027.

item.title: Teen goalkeeper Paulina ready for International Handball Federation Championship
item.description: At just 16 years old, Namosi Secondary School student Paulina Nagata is making her mark in handball, with the young goalkeeper relying on teamwork and communication as they prepare for the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship in Romania. The Year 11 student from Navunibau in Namosi says handball is similar to netball and football, making it an enjoyable and exci
item.content:encoded:
At just 16 years old, Namosi Secondary School student Paulina Nagata is making her mark in handball, with the young goalkeeper relying on teamwork and communication as they prepare for the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship in Romania.

The Year 11 student from Navunibau in Namosi says handball is similar to netball and football, making it an enjoyable and exciting sport to play.

Nagata says handball is mainly played by women and has helped her develop new skills and techniques.

Playing as a goalkeeper, she says communication is one of her biggest responsibilities on the court.

Nagata says she must always be prepared and work closely with her six teammates by constantly communicating with them, organising the defence and ensuring everyone is in the right position during a match.

She believes good teamwork and communication are essential for success and hopes more young people, especially girls, will take up handball and enjoy the opportunities the sport has to offer.

The 2026 International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship, will kick off tomorrow at the Kowloon Park Sports Centre in Hong Kong, with Group A matches scheduled over the opening five days of competition.

item.description: FIFA has launched an investigation into the altercation involving Argentina players and coaching staff following their 1-0 defeat

item.description: When it comes to the Fiji Rugby Union, there is always going to be a lot of rumours, opinions, and views.FRU Chair John Sanday sta

item.description: The unwavering support of parents has played a vital role in Nasinu Secondary School's Under-19 rugby team's success after defeati

item.title: Legge wants packed stadium for Drua Women’s title decider against Waratahs on Saturday
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women head coach Mike Legge has called on fans to turn out in force for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s final,
